title: Helper differentiation and second route opportunity phase
summary: Lane 4 should give one non-route-marker helper a real in-field difference, then add a second tactile living-world opportunity seam on another route family.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the non-route-marker helper differentiation handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Helper differentiation still needs an in-field proof, not just a map proof.

execution_order.goal: Implement the non-route-marker helper differentiation pass.
execution_order.why_now: At least one other helper should change how a route feels in the field.

execution_order.goal: Review the non-route-marker helper differentiation pass.
execution_order.why_now: The helper row should become more meaningful without growing.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the second tactile route-opportunity handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The living-world opportunity system should prove itself on more than one route family.

execution_order.goal: Implement the second tactile route-opportunity seam.
execution_order.why_now: A second route proof will make the replay system feel intentional instead of one-off.

execution_order.goal: Review the second tactile route-opportunity seam.
execution_order.why_now: Replay should deepen route feel without confusing route identity.

guardrails: No new support slots, inventory, or crafting layer.
Prefer clue visibility, targeting, or route emphasis over longer copy.
If a helper still cannot be made distinct, simplify instead of expanding.

main_269_focus.copy_goal: make hand-lens feel different in the field by preferring a notebook-fit clue over a nearby non-route decoy when both are inspectable
implementation_shape: Collect the current in-range inspectables instead of only the nearest one.
When hand-lens is selected, prefer the nearest in-range inspectable whose entry resolves through getHandLensNotebookFit(...).
Reuse that preferred target for both the field inspect marker and the keyboard inspect action.
If no notebook-fit clue is in range, fall back to the current nearest-entity behavior.
guardrails: Do not change click-to-inspect behavior for explicit entity clicks.
Do not widen inspect range or alter accepted route evidence ids.
Keep the pass helper-sized and reversible.

main_270_focus.copy_goal: make Held Sand change a real route opportunity by letting the back-dune pioneer stage resolve on a second truthful live clue during the replay window
implementation_shape: Extend scrub-edge-pattern processFocus.activeSlotEntryIdsBySlotId so Held Sand adds beach-grass to open-pioneer.
Rely on the existing route-progress and hand-lens resolver path instead of adding a new special-case advancement helper.
Keep notebook-ready and filed Scrub Pattern identity stable outside the active route step.
guardrails: Only affect scrub-edge-pattern during the active Held Sand replay window.
Keep the change on the opening back-dune stage only.
Do not widen the replay system beyond activeSlotEntryIdsBySlotId.
Do not rewrite station-side copy unless a focused test proves it is needed.
